title: Charlie McAvoy’s reward as Bruins’ OT hero? Another trip to the dentist’s chair.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Charlie McAvoy scored the OT winner for the Boston Bruins in a 2-1 victory over the Los Angeles Kings.
McAvoy faces ongoing dental issues, including a shattered jaw and lost teeth, after recent hits during games.
The Bruins are one point behind Detroit for the top wild-card spot, with McAvoy playing a crucial role.

title: After her son was struck by a car, she’s running the Boston Marathon for Newton firefighters
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Maureen Murphy is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Newton Firefighters Children's Fund.
Murphy's son, Teddy, was struck by a car two years ago, and Newton firefighters were first responders.
Murphy aims to raise funds for NFD, honoring their support during her family's difficult recovery.

title: Victor Wembanyama shines, Jaylen Brown is ejected as Celtics drop chaotic loss to Spurs: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Victor Wembanyama scored 39 points, hitting 8 of 15 three-pointers, leading the Spurs to a 125-116 victory over the Celtics.
Jaylen Brown was ejected after receiving two technical fouls from officials, impacting the Celtics' scoring abilities against the Spurs.
Derrick White excelled for the Celtics, scoring 34 points and effectively challenging Wembanyama in the paint and from three-point range.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (March 11)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: In Abington, a one-family Colonial at 353 Plymouth St., built in 1900, sold for $555,000 on a 26,553-square-foot lot.
A two-family conventional home in Allston at 22 Royal St., built in 1900, sold for $1,202,000 on a 2,528-square-foot lot.
Boston's 46 Chestnut St. row-middle home, built in 1900, sold for $22,000,000, consisting of 18 rooms and 6 bedrooms.

title: Bruins’ Charlie McAvoy scores winning goal. And then it was time to see the dentist — again.
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Charlie McAvoy scored the game-winning goal in overtime for the Bruins against the Los Angeles Kings, securing a 2-1 victory.
After the game, McAvoy needed dental work due to losing teeth in a second-period on-ice collision.
This win marked the Bruins' 13th consecutive home victory, maintaining their position in the playoff race.

title: Jaylen Brown blasts refs on social media after being ejected from Spurs game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected in the second quarter of the Celtics-Spurs game after receiving two technical fouls.
Following his ejection, Brown criticized the officiating on social media, expressing frustration with referee calls during the game.
Brown's absence contributed to the Celtics' 125-116 loss against the Spurs, with key players Pritchard and Vucevic also unavailable.

title: Man sentenced for fatally stabbing Chelsea woman, 59
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Angel Alvarez, 68, sentenced to life with a possibility of parole after 17 years for killing Margarita Morehead.
Alvarez pleaded guilty to second-degree murder, assault with a weapon, and violating a protection order.
Morehead was fatally stabbed in a Chelsea apartment in 2023, despite an active restraining order against Alvarez.

title: MBTA police seeking suspect who allegedly spat on, hit rider
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: An alleged assault occurred on Feb. 27 at 8 p.m. at the MBTA's Prudential station on the Green Line.
The suspect allegedly spat on and hit the rider multiple times with a closed fist, according to Transit Police.
MBTA police are seeking public assistance to identify the suspect, offering anonymous tips via 617-222-1050.

title: Maine man allegedly shot and killed girlfriend; pair worked together at ski resort
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Austin Doucette, 24, was arrested for allegedly killing his girlfriend, Makayla Rose DeSantis, 23, in Carrabassett Valley, Maine.
DeSantis was shot at an employee condo and later died at Maine Medical Center in Portland, police reported.
The couple worked at Sugarloaf Mountain Ski Resort; Doucette is held without bail as police continue the investigation.

title: Patriots reportedly remain interested in A.J. Brown trade after signing Romeo Doubs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots signed Romeo Doubs, a former Packers receiver, to a four-year contract worth up to $80 million.
New England remains interested in trading for Eagles' A.J. Brown despite adding Doubs to their roster.
Philadelphia demands a high price for Brown, possibly a 2027 first-round and 2026 second-round pick.
